channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Rick Pitino wins 1st Big East Coach of the Year award and RJ Luis Jr. is Player of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rick Pitino won his first Big East Coach of the Year award Wednesday and RJ Luis Jr. took Player of the Year honors after they propelled a resurgent St. John's program to its best regular season in almost four decades.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cheating scandal shocks ski jumping, topples Olympic champions and shakes Norway's lofty reputation
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Sign stealing in baseball. Match fixing in soccer. Doping allegations in swimming. Now ski jumping has its own scandal that escalated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utes knocked out of Big 12 Tournament by UCF
item.description: KANSAS CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a tumultuous season for the Runnin' Utes, and it most likely came to an end Tuesday night with an 87-72 loss to UCF in the first round of the Big 12 Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Injured Brad Marchand returns to the Boston ice with the Panthers
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Brad Marchand stepped back onto the ice at the TD Garden on Tuesday — this time in a Panthers practice jersey as the longtime Bruin returned to Boston for the first time since he was traded to Florida.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boys Lacrosse: Carlsbad 11, Granite Hills 7
item.description: EL CAJON (KUSI)- Boys Lacrosse action on Monday night in El Cajon with Granite Hills hosting Carlsbad. The Lancers win a very good game 11-7. Despite Carson Kowalsky, just a junior having a great game, scoring four goals for Granite Hills.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Dave Portnoy reveals 'weird' government call offering job; says he would've had to stop running Barstool
item.description: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy revealed a government call he got offering a job, where he would've had to stop running his media brand. He described the call as "weird.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big 12 commissioner believes NCAA Tournament expansion is due. He says 76 teams is the right number
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Big 12 commissioner Brett Yormark is in favor of expanding the men's NCAA Tournament to 76 teams, and he believes a decision could come in the next few months, opening the possibility of proposed changes being implemented as soon as next year.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warriors' Steph Curry becomes investor in Unrivaled Basketball
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Stephen Curry has joined Unrivaled basketball as an investor. The Golden State Warriors guard is the latest sports star to join the 3v3 basketball league as an investor, joining the likes of the NBA's Giannis Antetokounmpo and Carmelo Anthony, and tennis star Coco Gauff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Report: Bears sign center Drew Dalman to 3-year deal
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears have signed top free agent center Drew Dalman, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter. The 26-year-old former Falcon completes an overhaul of the Bears' interior offensive line after Poles and company jumped the market by trading for guards Joe Thuney and Jonah Jackson.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Colorado Springs upset CMU Mavericks to secure RMAC Championship
item.description: UC Colorado Springs defeated Colorado Mesa 63-61 to win the RMAC championship, with Amyah Moore Allen scoring 28 points and Olivia Reed Thyne scoring 18 points.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Chiefs WR Xavier Worthy released after prosecutor declines charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Utes win another NCAA skiing national title
item.description: HANOVER, N.H. (ABC4 Sports) - The University of Utah returned to the pinnacle of the collegiate skiing world on Saturday as the Utes won their 17th national title all-time—and fifth in the last six seasons—with the conclusion of the 2025 NCAA Championships.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Blackhawks in action against the Predators following overtime victory
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (20-35-8, in the Central Division) vs. Nashville Predators (23-32-7, in the Central Division) Nashville, Tennessee; Saturday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah keeps rolling with 4-2 victory at Detroit
item.description: DETROIT (ABC4 Sports) - Karel Vejmelka made 38 saves for the Utah Hockey Club, who scored three straight goals to rally for a 4-2 win against the Detroit Red Wings at Little Caesars Arena on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utes hire Alex Jensen as head basketball coach
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Alex Jensen is coming home to Utah. Jensen was hired as the University of Utah's 17th head basketball coach Thursdaay, replacing Craig Smith, who was fired last month.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Special Olympics Illinois athletes head to Italy for snowshoe races
item.description: On Wednesday, a team of Special Olympics Illinois snowshoe athletes boarded a plane to New York, with a final destination of Turin, Italy. There they will compete in the Special Olympics Winter World Games.
item.dc:creator: Erin McElroy, NewsNation
